key_features_for_minecraft.three_towers: West Tower, Mid Tower, East Tower - share a 6-story plinth then separate and shift
key_features_for_minecraft.tower_shift: At ~90m above ground the towers shift a few meters in different directions
key_features_for_minecraft.plinth_height: 30m shared base with 8.5m high entrance hall
key_features_for_minecraft.facade: 50,000m2 glass with vertical aluminum mullions, floor-to-ceiling windows
key_features_for_minecraft.programs: Offices (72,000m2), 240 apartments, 278-room hotel, congress hall, restaurants, 650-car parking

image_sources.description: Exterior view of De Rotterdam from across the Maas river, showing the three shifted towers
image_sources.useful_for: Overall massing and proportions of the three towers, understanding the stacking concept

image_sources.description: De Rotterdam south side view showing tower arrangement and facade grid
image_sources.useful_for: South elevation - good for understanding tower offset pattern from the side

image_sources.description: De Rotterdam at night showing illuminated glass facade and tower silhouettes
image_sources.useful_for: Night lighting reference, shows how the three towers read as separate volumes

image_sources.description: Video of Rem Koolhaas speaking about De Rotterdam tower design
image_sources.useful_for: Background on design intent and tower shifting concept

image_sources.useful_for: High-quality professional photos from multiple angles showing tower offsets and facade detail

architectural_drawings.notes: Scroll through full gallery - drawings are interspersed with photos

minecraft_build_notes.scale_suggestion: At 1:2 scale the building would be ~75 blocks tall, 50 blocks wide. At 1:4 it would be ~37 blocks tall, 25 wide.
minecraft_build_notes.key_challenge: The three towers share a base then shift at the midpoint - this offset is the defining feature
minecraft_build_notes.facade_pattern: Vertical aluminum mullions with floor-to-ceiling glass - use light gray concrete or iron bars for mullions, light blue/cyan glass for windows
minecraft_build_notes.tower_shift_detail: At approximately floor 22-25 (of 44), each tower shifts a few blocks in a different direction
minecraft_build_notes.color_palette: Silver/light gray aluminum framing + blue-tinted glass. The plinth base is slightly different treatment.
minecraft_build_notes.recommended_blocks: Light gray concrete, iron bars, light blue stained glass, cyan stained glass, smooth stone
